item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The item numbers in the header are wrong. This is 3853, but the header shows 3453. Sending ch00bakka a PM as well as posting here to let them know, as they may not look here for a while.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would slim down the Description section a little… it can be quite the trial to get past "what is the SCP" to "why is it anomalous? What's weird about these titles."

Agreed that the Foundation might not be this stupid; but equally, wouldn't be the stupidest thing they've done. As an idea for something on this point: did the Foundation have people in place (such as Ethics Committee/psychs?) to try and monitor the megalomania? What happened to them?

Streamline the piece a bit and it's a definite +1. Loved the correspondence with the O5 council.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think it would work wonderfully as a tale, but I'm not so sure as a skip. Also, the trap was too obvious for me to believe the Foundation had fall for it.

Maybe it would work better with less reliance from the Foundation's part on it, but keeping the overall form as the containment of this skip. Indeed, it could elaborate with brevity on skips taken from being previously contained by the Emperor or the Pontifex.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For a variety of reasons, I am loving this like a fat boy loves cake. The whole thing is delicious, and I can't decide which part is my favorite.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Instead of working on my senior thesis about ancient rome i wrote an scp about ancient rome and that about sums up my college experience.

Subtitled "This Is Why We Don't Contain Anomalies With Other Anomalies".
